data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`), but the `GenerateSampleCode` target never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile Include>` item after writing the file.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs before `CoreCompile` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild needs to know about the file as a compile item. The target needs to add an `<ItemGroup>` with a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` so the C# compiler picks it up.

Let me fix the `.csproj` file.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the MSBuild `Compile` item group. The C# compiler only compiles files it knows about through that item group.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
